response.justification: Candidate brings genuine geospatial domain expertise and demonstrated ability to work across diverse technical stacks, backed by strong seniority and current role at Google. However, the critical gap is the absence of modern C++ or Rust experience—the role explicitly requires this, and the resume shows only older C++ work (2015) and recent focus on TypeScript/Node.js. While the candidate's track record suggests capability to quickly ramp up, the technical requirements mismatch is significant enough to warrant exploring this gap before proceeding.
strengths: Deep geospatial and GIS expertise with real-world applications (Open Green Map platform, GISCollective); directly relevant to routing tile development
Proven ability to design and operate distributed systems handling scale (Dunnhumby: millions of requests/second; current role at Google across complex infrastructure)
Strong software craftsmanship emphasis (TDD, testing frameworks, code quality) aligns with the rigorous quality assurance required for routing systems
concerns: No modern C++ experience—only dated work from 2014-2015; role explicitly requires modern C++ as primary language, not alternative
No Rust experience; missing both primary and acceptable alternative technical requirements for a systems programming role
Domain expertise is in GIS/mapping data, not navigation systems or routing optimization; unclear familiarity with routing algorithms, tile structures, or traffic integration
